tools.name: anvil_current_user
tools.description: Get the authenticated Anvil user (from the API key) and the organizations they belong to (eid, name, slug). Good first call to discover org slugs/eids for other tools. GraphQL query: currentUser.

tools.name: anvil_get_organization
tools.description: Fetch an Anvil organization and its PDF templates (casts) and workflows (welds). Provide either its slug or its eid (at least one is required). GraphQL query: organization.

tools.name: anvil_get_cast
tools.description: Fetch a cast — an Anvil PDF template — by its eid: title, version number, and whether it is a template. Use the returned eid as the cast_eid for anvil_fill_pdf. GraphQL query: cast.

tools.name: anvil_get_etch_packet
tools.description: Fetch an Etch e-signature packet by eid: its status, details URL, document group, and every signer (eid, aliasId, name, email, status, routing order). Use this to check signature progress. GraphQL query: etchPacket.

tools.name: anvil_get_weld
tools.description: Fetch a weld — an Anvil workflow — by eid, including its forges (the individual form steps: eid, name, slug). GraphQL query: weld.

tools.name: anvil_get_weld_data
tools.description: Fetch a weldData record — a single submission to a weld (workflow) — by eid: completion state, test flag, and creation time. GraphQL query: weldData.

tools.name: anvil_fill_pdf
tools.description: Fill an Anvil PDF template (cast) with data and return the finished PDF as base64. REST: POST /fill/{castEid}.pdf. Returns { filename, byteLength, pdfBase64 }. The `data` object's keys are the template's field aliases.

tools.name: anvil_generate_pdf
tools.description: Generate a brand-new PDF from HTML or Markdown and return it as base64. REST: POST /generate-pdf. For type=html, pass data = { html, css? }. For type=markdown, pass data = [ { label, content }, ... ]. Returns { filename, byteLength, pdfBase64 }.

tools.name: anvil_create_etch_packet
tools.description: Create an Etch e-signature packet (documents + signers). GraphQL mutation: createEtchPacket. IMPORTANT: isDraft defaults to true (a safe draft that sends nothing). Setting isDraft=false with real signers SENDS signature-request emails to those signers. Use isTest=true while developing.

tools.name: anvil_generate_etch_sign_url
tools.description: Generate a one-time embedded signing URL for a specific signer in an Etch packet (for iframe/redirect signing flows). GraphQL mutation: generateEtchSignURL. Returns { url }.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: anvil_get_etch_packet · Private data: anvil_current_user, anvil_get_etch_packet, anvil_get_weld_data, anvil_create_etch_packet · Public sink: anvil_get_etch_packet, anvil_fill_pdf, anvil_generate_pdf.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: anvil_get_etch_packet · Private data: anvil_current_user, anvil_get_etch_packet, anvil_get_weld_data, anvil_create_etch_packet · Public sink: anvil_get_etch_packet, anvil_fill_pdf, anvil_generate_pdf

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
